ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "screen_name" TEXT /* Screen name of the user */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the user */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the user's profile */,
  "verified" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user's account is verified */,
  "follower_count" INTEGER /* Number of followers the user has */,
  "following_count" INTEGER /* Number of users the user is following */,
  "profile_image_url" TEXT /* URL to the user's profile image */,
  "is_brand" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user is a brand or influencer */,
  "verified_date" TEXT /* Date the user's account was verified */,
  "account_created_at" TEXT /* Date the user's account was created */,
  "last_tweet_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last tweet */,
  "engagement_score" INTEGER /* Composite engagement score of the user */,
  "languages" TEXT /* List of languages the user frequently uses */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dog_types" (
  "dog_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dog type */,
  "dog_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the dog type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dog type */,
  "average_rating" REAL /* Average rating for the dog type */,
  "popularity_score" INTEGER /* Popularity score of the dog type */,
  "breed_characteristics" TEXT /* Description of the breed's characteristics */,
  "is_rare" INTEGER /* Indicates if the dog type is rare */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dog_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of source (e.g., mobile, web) */,
  "popularity_score" INTEGER /* Popularity score of the source */,
  "engagement_score" INTEGER /* Composite engagement score for the source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_engagement" (
  "engagement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engagement record */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "favorite_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the tweet has been favorited */,
  "retweet_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the tweet has been retweeted */,
  "comment_count" INTEGER /* Number of comments on the tweet */,
  "engagement_change" TEXT /* Change in engagement over time */,
  "engagement_rate" REAL /* Rate of engagement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engagement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_images" (
  "image_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each image */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet associated with the image */,
  "jpg_url" TEXT /* URL to the JPEG image */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the image */,
  "image_dimensions" TEXT /* Dimensions of the image (width and height) */,
  "image_format" TEXT /* Format of the image (e.g., JPEG, PNG) */,
  "image_tags" TEXT /* List of tags associated with the image */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("image_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet associated with the rating */,
  "rating_numerator" INTEGER /* Numerator of the rating */,
  "rating_denominator" INTEGER /* Denominator of the rating */,
  "rating_text" TEXT /* Text representation of the rating */,
  "rating_description" TEXT /* Description of the rating */,
  "rating_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the rating was given */,
  "rating_source" TEXT /* Source of the rating (e.g., user, automated system) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_dates" (
  "date_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each date record */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet associated with the date */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year when the tweet was created */,
  "month" INTEGER /* Month when the tweet was created */,
  "day" INTEGER /* Day when the tweet was created */,
  "hour" INTEGER /* Hour when the tweet was created */,
  "minute" INTEGER /* Minute when the tweet was created */,
  "second" INTEGER /* Second when the tweet was created */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("date_id")
)
